human_task_name: SynAIpse CC0: record failure-envelope compatibility boundary
objective: Map every SynaipseState, contextUnknowns, and decision-envelope producer and reader; choose the canonical versioned failure-envelope representation; and write the CC0 compatibility brief without changing runtime behavior.

decision_frame.value: Make CC1 and CC2 implement against one explicit, reversible contract boundary.
decision_frame.why_now: The clinical-delivery plan blocks runtime work until the producer-reader compatibility boundary is recorded from the current main baseline.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation brief names the source owner, public surface, producers, readers, and old/new compatibility matrix.
The brief keeps contextUnknowns restricted to optional identified ch_context failures.
The brief selects one versioned failure-envelope representation with migration and revert conditions.
dependencies: The isolated clinical-delivery planning worktree remains reference-only and unmodified.
decision_frame.risk_or_learning_target: Avoid silently overloading contextUnknowns or creating a second competing decision/effect envelope.
decision_frame.smallest_increment: One inspectable CC0 implementation brief plus Worker handoff and schema-valid child result.

non_goals: Do not modify runtime TypeScript, JSON schemas, generated artifacts, or tests.
Do not begin CC1 or CC2.
Do not add commands, search, index, memory, or hidden persistence systems.
Do not push, create a PR, mutate hosted services, commit, rebase, clean, or alter the primary checkout.
Do not edit the isolated planning worktree.

validation_entrypoints: pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/synaipse/context-contract.test.ts src/lib/synaipse/state.test.ts src/commands/next.test.ts --reporter=dot

delegated_decision_rights: Choose bounded read-only investigation order inside CC0.
Write only the declared CC0 evidence artifacts without changing runtime behavior.
Recommend, but do not implement, the first CC1 patch.
